heading: ## Risk matrix
body:
| Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|-------------|--------|------------|
| Low recruitment rate | Medium | High | Extend recruitment period by 3 months; activate backup sites (TBD) |
| High dropout rate (>20%) | Medium | High | Implement retention protocol: monthly check-ins, compensation for follow-up visits |
| LLM technical failure (downtime >48h) | Low | Medium | Redundant server deployment; fallback to standard care for affected participants |
| Adverse event rate exceeds stopping rule | Low | High | DSMB review; immediate halt and protocol revision if confirmed |
| Data integrity breach | Low | Critical | Encrypted storage; access logs; regular security audits |
| Confounding by seasonal effects | Medium | Low | Stratified randomisation by enrollment month; sensitivity analysis with season as covariate

heading: ## Limitations
body:
- Single-centre design limits generalisability to other healthcare settings and populations
- Open-label design (no sham intervention) may introduce performance bias; blinding of outcome assessors only
- PAM-13 is a self-reported measure, subject to social desirability and recall bias
- LLM intervention is a black-box system; reproducibility of LLM responses cannot be guaranteed across model updates
- Follow-up limited to 6 months; long-term effects on patient activation and clinical outcomes unknown
- Sample size calculated for primary endpoint only; secondary and exploratory analyses may be underpowered
- Assumed standard deviation (σ=10) is based on a single published study; pilot phase may reveal larger variance requiring sample size adjustment
